item.title: How to make an API call in the middle of an OpenAI conversation
item.author: Julia Shevchenko
item.content:encoded:
While AI integrations become more and more desirable features, the need to perform additional functionality, for example an API call, in the middle of the conversations is the biggest concern for developers. Even though it is possible to provide AI assistants with clear instructions, they sometimes struggle to follow them. The most problematic are if/else conditions. For demonstration purposes, I've created a console application that will simulate a chat with OpenAI via completion API. So, everything is put inside the background Worker class. We will use Azure.AI.OpenAI package to interact with the API. Completions API doesn't store the state, so we need to pass the chat history with every call. Chat history should consist of type. There are several chat types: (system) - use to provide system instructions that will be considered by OpenAI but won't be visible to the user; (user) - contains messages from the user; (assistant) - contains messages from OpenAI; (tool) - use to provide result of the tool (in our case function) call. - is a concept of additional chat functionality, in our case, a function. So, every function is a ChatTool.
Step 1. Starting a conversation First of all, we need to have system instructions to describe OpenAI's role in this conversation. Here, we can also tell OpenAI that we want to execute some function after specific user actions.In this example, I want it to be an assistant in a bookstore, and when the user picks up a book - execute a function that will call our API to get book information, check if there are any deals, and tell the user if there are any. So, when the user says, "I want to buy this book", we will make a call to our bookstore API, check if there are any, and send with new instructions. Doing the deal check on our backend will also help to avoid OpenAI misbehavior with if/else conditions.Inside such function calls, we can do whatever we need: call an API, do some calculations, send emails, etc.In the diagram, I show the part of the simplified flow from the user message that indicates a desire to buy a book to the new instruction for OpenAI. Let's now move on to the implementation.We will store message history in the property of type . ExecuteAsync is an entry point for our background worker. So, we are waiting for the user to type anything, then save the message and continue our conversation.Calling a function without any context would be useless. We need to have information about the book that user has chosen: name and author. For this, we need to define which fields we are expecting to receive from OpenAI. It can be done as the following:
Step 2. Continue Conversation To continue conversation, we will always send message history to the OpenAI API including functions definition if there are any.The response will always be a object with which could be one of the following: , , , and . We are interested in the first two. For simplification, others are omitted in this example. indicates that there is an ordinary response from OpenAI with some text. We need to store it as an . means that OpenAI identified that some Tool is required to be executed. It is our responsibility to execute it. The text from the response should be also stored in . And, in addition, each tool call result should be saved as . After and are sent, we need to , so OpenAI can react to new prompts. In this example, we will execute tool calls in method.
Step 3. Execute Tool Call In ExecuteToolCall we will define how to execute each function that we may receive. In this case, it's only one, but could be any amounts.Our goal here is to call our bookstore backend API to get information about the book that user wants to buy: is it available and does it have any deals that user should be notified about. is the method where we can perform anything we need to extend conversation logic. It is the actual implementation of the . Since toolCall.FunctionArguments is binary, we can convert it into string and deserialize it to the DTO. Then, it's time to call bookstore API to get book information. After that, depending on the type, add a system message.As you can see in the second step, the system message will be sent to the OpenAI to be considered.Let's see how the conversation will look like.For the selected book, the deal is 1+1=2, meaning buy one more book - get another one for free. OpenAI understands the user's requests and, according to the system instructions, kindly informs the user about this possibility and even asks if he wants to use it. I think that's a very accurate example of how businesses can benefit from OpenAI integration, especially when it is enhanced by custom functions calls. Have you already tried OpenAI or other integrations on your projects? Do you like how it performs?

item.title: The Perfect Prompt
item.author: Daniel T Sasser II
item.content:encoded: What if we cared enough To find the perfect words to say to people Just like we do the perfect prompt? What if we cared enough To savor the emotion of a conversation without being so tough? What if we cared enough To mind our words and manners the way we do when we're crafting a prompt? What if we cared enough To rewrite the code of how we interact and interrupt? To plant the seeds of gentleness, and watch the good erupt? The world could shift, and suddenly, a kinder future that doesn't corrupt.Daniel T. Sasser II is an AI researcher and software developer who builds intelligent systems to enhance human workflows. His work focuses on optimizing AI-assisted content creation, automation, and software development, bridging the gap between cutting-edge technology and real-world applications.Beyond code and computation, he finds solace in the art of creative writing, where structure meets spontaneity and logic gives way to emotion. For him, poetry is more than words. It is a reflection of the human element that is often missing in the world of algorithms.For more insights on AI and creative writing, follow Dan on his blog or Facebook. Join our Facebook group, AI Questions and Answers, to take part in the conversation. Let me know you enjoyed this work of art by dropping a comment below.

item.title: Nvidia’s Record-Breaking Earnings: How AI Demand and Data Centers Are Powering Its Growth
item.author: AI+Tech
item.content:encoded: Nvidia Corporation has once again demonstrated its dominance in the semiconductor industry, reporting exceptional financial results for the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2025. The company's performance has been significantly bolstered by the surging demand for artificial intelligence (AI) chips, particularly its latest Blackwell architecture, which has become a cornerstone in data centers worldwide.Financial Performance OverviewFor the quarter ending January 26, 2025, Nvidia reported a record revenue of $39.3 billion, marking a 78% increase from the same period last year and a 12% rise from the previous quarter. Net income reached $22.06 billion, surpassing analysts' expectations and reflecting the company's robust operational efficiency. Adjusted earnings per share stood at $0.89, exceeding the projected $0.85. The data center segment emerged as a significant contributor, generating $35.6 billion in revenue—a 93% increase year-over-year. This surge is primarily attributed to the widespread adoption of Nvidia's Blackwell AI chips by major cloud service providers and enterprises aiming to enhance their AI capabilities. Blackwell Architecture: A Game ChangerIntroduced in December 2024, the Blackwell architecture has rapidly gained traction in the AI and data center markets. In its debut quarter, Blackwell-related products accounted for $11 billion in revenue, marking the fastest product ramp in Nvidia's history. CEO Jensen Huang expressed enthusiasm about the overwhelming demand, stating, "We’ve successfully ramped up the massive-scale production of Blackwell AI supercomputers, achieving billions of dollars in sales in its first quarter." The Blackwell architecture, named after mathematician David Blackwell, represents a significant leap in AI processing power and efficiency. It integrates seamlessly with Nvidia's Grace CPU, offering a comprehensive solution for AI workloads. This combination has positioned Nvidia as a preferred partner for organizations seeking to deploy advanced AI applications. Impact on Data Centers and AI DemandThe proliferation of AI technologies has led to an increased demand for high-performance computing solutions, with data centers at the forefront of this transformation. Nvidia's GPUs have become integral in training and deploying AI models, enabling faster computations and more efficient data processing. The company's data center revenue now constitutes a substantial portion of its overall earnings, underscoring the critical role of AI in its growth strategy.Cloud service giants such as AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure have heavily invested in Nvidia's Blackwell-powered systems. These collaborations have facilitated the deployment of AI-driven services and applications, catering to a diverse range of industries from healthcare to finance. The scalability and performance of Nvidia's solutions have made them a cornerstone in modern data center infrastructures. Market Position and Future OutlookNvidia's strategic focus on AI and data center markets has propelled its market capitalization to over $3 trillion, solidifying its position as one of the most valuable companies globally. The company's forward-looking approach is evident in its plans to introduce the Blackwell Ultra chip in the latter half of 2025, promising enhanced performance and efficiency. This upcoming release is anticipated to further strengthen Nvidia's foothold in the AI hardware sector. Despite facing competition from emerging players like China's DeepSeek, which has developed cost-effective AI models, Nvidia's commitment to innovation and quality has maintained its leadership position. The company's ability to deliver cutting-edge technology that meets the evolving needs of AI applications continues to set it apart in the industry. Challenges and ConsiderationsWhile Nvidia's financial performance and market position are impressive, the company remains vigilant about potential challenges. Geopolitical factors, such as export controls and tariffs, could impact operations, especially concerning sales to regions like China. Additionally, the rapid pace of technological advancement necessitates continuous investment in research and development to stay ahead of competitors. Furthermore, the initial rollout of the Blackwell architecture encountered production complexities due to a design flaw, which has since been rectified. CEO Jensen Huang acknowledged the issue, noting that the transition required a new chassis architecture and power delivery system, making the shift from the previous Hopper architecture challenging. Addressing these production hurdles has been crucial in meeting the escalating demand for AI chips. Nvidia's exceptional fourth-quarter performance underscores its pivotal role in the AI and data center industries. The successful launch and rapid adoption of the Blackwell architecture have not only driven significant revenue growth but also reinforced Nvidia's reputation as a leader in technological innovation. As AI continues to permeate various sectors, Nvidia's strategic investments and product offerings position it well to capitalize on the expanding market opportunities.Investors and industry stakeholders will undoubtedly monitor Nvidia's developments closely, particularly with the anticipated release of the Blackwell Ultra chip and the company's efforts to navigate geopolitical and production challenges. Nvidia's trajectory suggests a sustained influence on the future landscape of AI and data center technologies.

item.title: Automating Kubernetes Cost Optimization with AI: The Next Frontier in DevOps
item.author: DevvEmeka
item.content:encoded:
Kubernetes has revolutionized cloud infrastructure by providing scalable and efficient container orchestration. However, managing in Kubernetes remains a challenge for DevOps teams. Resources are often , waste money, and manual cost optimization is time-consuming.Enter . By integrating machine learning and predictive analytics, DevOps teams can automate Kubernetes cost optimization, ensuring that clusters scale intelligently, resources are utilized efficiently, and costs are minimized without human intervention.In this article, we’ll explore how AI is reshaping Kubernetes cost management, walk through a real-world implementation, and discuss the today.
The Problem: Why Kubernetes Costs Are Hard to Control Most Kubernetes clusters waste 35-50% of their allocated resources, leading to unnecessary cloud expenses. This happens due to: Developers often request more CPU and memory than needed. HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler) reacts to immediate load but doesn't predict future needs. Underutilized nodes remain active, increasing costs. Manual optimization requires deep expertise and constant monitoring.
Traditional Cost Optimization Strategies (and Their Limitations) Before AI, teams relied on:Resource Requests & Limits – Setting hard limits on CPU and memory (manual and error-prone). – Adjusts nodes dynamically but lacks workload forecasting.Spot & Reserved Instances – Saves costs but still requires monitoring and intervention.These methods lack automation and predictive intelligence—this is where AI steps in.
How AI Automates Kubernetes Cost Optimization AI-driven cost optimization relies on that analyze , , and dynamically adjust resources. The key benefits include: Adjust resources traffic spikes. Recommends the for each pod.Automated Node Optimization: Identifies and removes . Uses AI models to predict .
AI-Driven Cost Optimization Tools Several tools are available to implement AI-based cost optimization:Real-time monitoring and AI-driven cost recommendationsIntelligent node autoscaler (AWS, EKS, GKE)ML-powered power consumption tracking for KubernetesVPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) + AIPredictive resource adjustments for pods
Implementing AI-Driven Cost Optimization in Kubernetes (AWS Example) Let’s walk through a : AI-powered for automatic node scaling on .Step 1: Install Karpenter on AWS EKShelm repo add karpenter https://charts.karpenter.sh helm repo update helm karpenter karpenter/karpenter karpenter Step 2: Define an AI-Optimized Scaling PolicyKarpenter leverages to analyze node utilization and automatically spin up the right instance types.This configuration the most cost-efficient AWS EC2 instances for Kubernetes workloads.Step 3: Enable AI-Driven Workload ForecastingTo predict resource demand, we integrate with :kubectl apply https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubecost/cost-model/main/manifests/kubecost.yaml Kubecost will analyze past and provide AI-based recommendations for cost savings.
Real-World Example: How a SaaS Company Saved 40% on Kubernetes Costs A SaaS company running high-traffic applications on AWS EKS faced . After implementing AI-driven cost optimization using Karpenter and Kubecost:Unused nodes were automatically removed, reducing idle costs.AI-based predictions scaled resources efficiently, preventing over-provisioning.Overall Kubernetes costs dropped by 40% in just three months.
The Future of AI in Kubernetes Cost Optimization The next wave of AI-powered Kubernetes cost optimization will include:✅ – AI will detect anomalies and auto-recover failed pods. ✅ Multi-Cloud AI Optimization – Dynamic cost balancing across AWS, GCP, and Azure. ✅ – Fine-tuned predictions at .AI-driven cost optimization is no longer a luxury—it’s a necessity for DevOps teams managing cloud-native Kubernetes applications. By leveraging , , and real-time cost monitoring, organizations can reduce cloud expenses, improve efficiency, and scale seamlessly.✔️ AI can predict and optimize Kubernetes costs automatically. ✔️ Tools like Karpenter and Kubecost make AI-powered scaling easy. ✔️ AI-driven autoscaling reduces over-provisioning and idle costs.By implementing these AI-powered techniques, you can future-proof your Kubernetes infrastructure while keeping cloud costs under control. What are your biggest challenges in optimizing Kubernetes costs? Drop a comment below!

item.title: Canary Software: A Smart Approach to Deployment
item.author: keploy
item.content:encoded: In modern software development, deploying new features and updates can be risky, especially in production environments. A faulty release can cause downtime, impact user experience, and result in financial losses. Canary software deployment is a progressive release strategy that helps teams mitigate these risks by testing new features on a small subset of users before rolling them out to everyone. This method allows developers to monitor performance, gather real-time feedback, and ensure stability before a full-scale release.Canary software refers to a deployment strategy where a new version of an application is initially released to a limited group of users. The term "canary" comes from the historical practice of using canaries in coal mines to detect harmful gases—if the canary was affected, miners knew there was danger. Similarly, in software, a canary release helps detect issues before they impact all users.This strategy is particularly useful in cloud-native and microservices architectures, where frequent updates are required, and software reliability is critical.Why is Canary Deployment Important?Canary deployments reduce the risks associated with software releases by providing real-world feedback before a broader rollout. Some key advantages include: – Only a small group of users is exposed to potential issues. – Developers can identify and fix issues before they reach all users. – Ensures that new updates are stable and meet user expectations. – If a problem is detected, the team can quickly revert to the previous version.How Canary Deployment WorksThe canary deployment process follows a structured approach to ensure controlled releases: – A fraction of users (e.g., 5-10%) gets access to the new update. – Teams collect performance metrics, error logs, and user feedback. – If the update performs well, the deployment gradually expands to more users. – If issues arise, the update is rolled back to the previous stable version.This process allows for a smooth transition while minimizing the impact of potential failures.Benefits of Canary Software DeploymentCanary deployments offer several benefits, making them a preferred choice for software teams: – Exposes only a small segment of users to potential failures. – Developers can track application health and user behavior before a full release. – If something goes wrong, rolling back to the previous version is easy. – Helps in fine-tuning application behavior based on live feedback.Challenges in Canary DeploymentWhile canary deployments are highly effective, they come with certain challenges: – Requires robust observability tools to track key performance indicators (KPIs). – Needs automation and traffic routing capabilities to manage different software versions. – Selecting the right subset of users for initial deployment is crucial.Canary Deployment vs. Other Release StrategiesCanary deployment is just one of several software release strategies. Here's how it compares to other methods:Gradual release to small groupsDeploy new version alongside the old oneIncrementally update instancesCanary deployments strike a balance between risk and efficiency, making them ideal for continuous delivery environments.Tools for Implementing Canary DeploymentsSeveral tools facilitate canary deployments, ensuring smooth execution: – Supports progressive deployment strategies using service mesh tools. – Automates API testing and regression checks before deploying new features, ensuring reliability. – Provide real-time monitoring and alerting for canary instances. – Enable intelligent traffic routing and observability for microservices.Keploy plays a crucial role in canary deployments by automatically generating test cases and ensuring that updates do not introduce regressions. By leveraging Keploy, developers can validate their API changes before rolling out updates, reducing the risk of failures.Best Practices for Canary DeploymentTo maximize success with canary deployment, teams should follow these best practices: – Establish key performance indicators (KPIs) such as error rates, response times, and user feedback to evaluate deployment performance. – Set up automated rollback mechanisms in case issues arise. – Control releases dynamically without requiring redeployments. – Collect and analyze feedback from canary users to refine updates.Leverage Automated Testing – Use tools like Keploy to automate API testing before deployment.Canary software deployment is a powerful strategy for releasing software updates with minimal risk. By gradually introducing changes, monitoring performance, and leveraging automated testing tools like Keploy, teams can ensure smooth and reliable deployments. Implementing a well-structured canary deployment process helps organizations maintain high application stability while continuously delivering new features to users.

item.title: [Boost]
item.author: Ifedamola Adefisoye

item.title: Codeless Test Automation: The Future of Software Testing
item.author: Vaibhav Kulshrestha
item.content:encoded:
Software testing has always been an essential component of the software development lifecycle (SDLC). It ensures that applications meet quality standards, function correctly, and provide a seamless user experience before reaching production environments. Over the decades, testing methodologies have evolved significantly, keeping pace with the changing landscape of software development. Initially, software testing was entirely manual, requiring human testers to execute test cases, compare actual results with expected outcomes, and document defects. While this approach worked for small-scale applications, it quickly became a bottleneck as software systems grew in complexity and scale.With the rise of automation, testing processes became more efficient. Automated testing introduced scripting languages such as Selenium, Appium, and Cypress, enabling testers to create scripts that could execute repetitive test cases automatically. This marked a significant shift, reducing human effort, increasing test coverage, and accelerating the development cycle. However, traditional test automation came with its own set of challenges. Writing and maintaining automation scripts required programming expertise, which created dependencies on skilled developers. Additionally, frequent software updates often resulted in broken scripts, leading to high maintenance efforts and costs.To address these challenges, codeless test automation has emerged as a game-changer in the software testing domain. By eliminating the need for manual coding, codeless automation democratizes test automation, making it accessible to non-technical users, including business analysts, product managers, and manual testers. This approach simplifies automation, accelerates test execution, and enhances collaboration within Agile and DevOps teams.This article explores the concept of codeless test automation, its importance, the technologies that enable it, its benefits, challenges, real-world applications, and the future of software testing in a rapidly evolving digital landscape.
The Growing Need for Codeless Test Automation Challenges of Traditional Test Automation Traditional test automation has significantly improved the efficiency of software testing, but it comes with several limitations that hinder its widespread adoption and effectiveness.1️⃣ High Dependency on Programming Skills Traditional test automation requires testers to have programming knowledge in languages like Java, Python, or JavaScript. Many organizations struggle to find testers with both testing expertise and coding proficiency, making automation adoption slower and more expensive.2️⃣ Time-Consuming Test Script Development Creating and maintaining automated test scripts is a time-intensive process. Even for experienced automation engineers, writing test scripts for complex workflows takes considerable effort. Moreover, minor changes in the application often require script updates, increasing maintenance time.3️⃣ Fragility of Test Scripts Automated test scripts are often brittle and break when the application’s UI changes. If a button, field, or navigation element is modified, the corresponding test script fails, requiring frequent updates. This makes traditional automation unreliable for fast-paced Agile and DevOps environments.4️⃣ Limited Collaboration Since traditional automation relies heavily on coding, it creates a divide between technical testers and non-technical stakeholders (such as business analysts and product managers). This limits collaboration and prevents cross-functional teams from actively participating in test automation efforts.5️⃣ Slow Adaptation to Agile and CI/CD Pipelines Modern software development follows Agile methodologies and Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) practices. Traditional automation struggles to keep pace with rapid iterations, frequent software releases, and continuous testing requirements.These challenges have fueled the demand for a more efficient, accessible, and scalable approach to test automation, leading to the rise of codeless test automation.
Understanding Codeless Test Automation What is Codeless Test Automation? Codeless test automation refers to an approach where testers can create, execute, and manage automated test cases without writing code. Instead of scripting, codeless tools provide visual interfaces, drag-and-drop functionalities, AI-driven testing, and natural language processing (NLP) to enable test automation.How Codeless Automation Works Most codeless automation tools follow a simple workflow:✅ – Users interact with the application, and the tool records their actions to create automated test cases.✅ Drag-and-Drop Test Creation – Users build test cases by dragging predefined components into a workflow, without writing scripts.✅ AI-Powered Self-Healing Tests – AI automatically updates test scripts when UI elements change, reducing maintenance efforts.✅ – Test cases can be written in plain English, making automation accessible to non-technical users.✅ Integration with CI/CD Pipelines – Codeless tools integrate with DevOps workflows, enabling continuous testing.Popular Codeless Automation Tools Some of the most widely used codeless automation tools include:🔹 – AI-driven test automation with self-healing capabilities.🔹 – Low-code and codeless testing for web, mobile, and API testing.🔹 – Visual-based automation with AI-enhanced test execution.🔹 – Model-based testing with end-to-end automation capabilities.
The Benefits of Codeless Test Automation 🚀 1. Faster Test Creation & Execution Codeless automation allows teams to build and execute tests rapidly, significantly reducing the time required for automation.🔧 2. Lower Maintenance Costs AI-driven self-healing mechanisms adapt to UI changes, reducing the need for frequent script updates.👥 3. Enhanced Collaboration Business analysts, product managers, and manual testers can participate in test automation, eliminating dependency on developers.🎯 Since tests are easier to create, more test scenarios can be automated, increasing overall coverage and reducing manual testing efforts.💰 5. Reduced Skill Barriers Codeless tools require minimal coding knowledge, enabling organizations to scale automation without hiring specialized automation engineers.🔄 6. Seamless Integration with DevOps & CI/CD Codeless automation integrates smoothly with Jenkins, GitLab, Azure DevOps, and other CI/CD tools, enabling continuous testing.
Challenges & Limitations of Codeless Automation Despite its advantages, codeless test automation has certain limitations:🔸 – Some complex test scenarios require scripting, making hybrid approaches necessary.🔸 – Organizations may become dependent on a specific tool with limited flexibility to switch.🔸 – AI-powered self-healing is still evolving and may not always make correct updates.Organizations must carefully evaluate their requirements before adopting a codeless automation tool to ensure it aligns with their long-term testing strategy.
Real-World Applications of Codeless Test Automation Codeless automation is widely used across industries, including:🏦 – Automating transaction workflows, API testing, and security compliance tests.🛒 – Testing payment gateways, shopping carts, and user experiences across devices.🚗 – Validating infotainment systems, autonomous driving software, and IoT integrations.📱 – Ensuring seamless performance and functionality across Android & iOS devices.
The Future of Codeless Test Automation As AI, machine learning, and natural language processing continue to advance, codeless test automation will become even more intelligent, adaptive, and scalable. Future innovations may include:🔹 AI-powered autonomous testing🔹 Voice-assisted test case creation🔹 Blockchain-powered security validation🔹 Cloud-based global test executionCodeless automation is set to redefine software testing, making quality assurance faster, more efficient, and more accessible than ever before.Codeless test automation is not just a trend—it is the future of software testing. By eliminating the need for complex scripting, it empowers organizations to achieve faster releases, reduce costs, and enhance software quality. Businesses that embrace codeless automation today will gain a competitive advantage in tomorrow’s digital landscape.

item.title: Top 7 Sites To Buy Verified PayPal Accounts In This Year
item.author: Cuty Pie
item.content:encoded: Buy Verified PayPal Accounts In the world where online business is expanding greatly, PayPal is considered to be one of the most famous and used money transfer companies. PayPal was developed in 1998 as an e-commerce businessman’s tool for securely sealing fund transfers and receiving money. Whether it is in exchange for goods, for services, or for cash transfer from one country to another PayPal just makes it more convenient by being a middleman between the buyer and the seller.The PayPal accounts are the personal PayPal accounts; PayPal business accounts The other feature which has an impact on usability is the verification of the account. Having a PayPal account can prove the authenticity and credibility of an account holder and having a verified account gives extra bonus like higher limits on the amount one is allowed to send or receive through PayPal account, less probability of hold or freeze on accounts among others.What is a Verified PayPal Account? Now let’s proceed to an understanding of what it actually means to have a verified PayPal account and where you can safely go to attain them. Even PayPal whose main service delivery is based on financial transaction follows stringent identity verification requirements to safely and legally allow its clients’ usage. A basic PayPal account or a Personal PayPal account is an account that lets you both, send and receive money but has some restrictions. For example, an undeveloped account will have a limited transaction capacity, limited account functionality, and can attract attention during high-value transactions.A verified PayPal account does not suffer such limitations. It means the user has linked his/her valid PayPal account with a source of financing say an account number in a bank or a credit card. By establishing this dual layer of identification, PayPal can confidence the account holder’s identity more and can provide more safety attributes and higher transaction capabilities.Verification ensures: Higher transaction limits.Increased customer or business partner trust or increases the institution’s perceived legitimacy.Increased safety measures and a decreased chance of getting an account closed.However, for those who engage in the use of the e-service, the verification process may be a little discouraging. Many nations do not have an opportunity to open a bank account or use financial services that PayPal accepts. This has created a market of people who want to obtain ready-made verified accounts.Why People Buy Verified PayPal Accounts There are several reasons why people may opt to buy verified PayPal accounts rather than creating and verifying their own:Convenience: For many users, the process of verification might be even unreasonably annoying or they can experience some problems with fulfilling the conditions, especially in case if they are from countries with more stringent financial policy.Faster Setup: Merchants that highly depend on PayPal for their payments prefer a PayPal verified account from the beginning. One may be purchased and this can enable the performance of operations as soon as it is bought.Trust and Credibility: PayPal verified account is a plus to other business partners and customers because it is come along with certain background check and banking verifications.Higher Transaction Limits: Business accounts in general have higher, sending, receiving, and withdrawing limits than unverified PayPal accounts.Avoiding Regional Restrictions: PayPal’s functionality is somewhat restricted by country, and purchasing a verified PayPal account built in a nation with fewer limitations can help.Where to Buy Verified PayPal Accounts Safely PayPal is one of the most secure payment services in the world, especially now when people deal with online payments. It is still employed by so many people, whether buyers or sellers of goods and services for convenience and safety. However, some users may be content and willing to use unverified PayPal account, for convenience or for other reason, there are few who would like to have PayPal account that has been verified. If you’re interested in where to purchase authentic PayPal accounts without getting a scam, this article will give you an overview of where to find the accounts, precautions,guidelines to keep you safe from fraudulent individuals.These are the reliable Marketplaces as well as vendors in fields of lifestyle products and electronics. This is why it’s very important to find a proper marketplace or a seller who would be able to offer you a verified PayPal account in the event you are extremely decided to do that. There are many websites that have lists of sellers that offer these accounts, but often it is a trap. Focuses on the marketplaces buyer feedbacks are good, has a policy that protects buyers, and has high credibility. While companies like Fame swap and Player Up provide trading of accounts most of the time, PayPal accounts are not the type of accounts they mainly deal in. Never leave a room without checking the reviews because they are so many fake Vendors out there.Escrow Services for SecurityUse of independent escrow services is probably the safest bet if you are interested in purchasing verified PayPal accounts. An escrow service provider is involved in a transaction and only releases payment once you are both content with the deal. Situation at companies like Escrow.com – here the buyer pays only when the account has been delivered according to the agreed terms and conditions. This greatly lowers the likelihood of being scammed or the receipt of a nefarious account.Social media selling or selling on forums-platforms Sometimes you can buy a verified PayPal account from an individual seller in forums and groups on Reddit, FB or similar websites. Although this process may appear to be more effective and can be less costly it is risky. Thus, it would be nice to check carefully everything concerning the seller – the reputation, feedback, and previous deals with the seller. Seller should avoid requests for direct payment transactions other than through well-known sites, as this is dangerous.Important of Buy PayPal Accounts PayPal is one of the most recognizable and popular payment systems for the transfer of funds through digital devices, doubling as one of the most used systems for making daily payments on an international level. Regardless of the use for personal or business uses, having a verified PayPal account ensures hassle free and secure means of making or receiving payments anywhere in the world. As such, businesses need to purchase PayPal accounts as many companies aim to make improvements and enhancements on their online business. This article focuses on the subject of purchasing PayPal accounts, especially to those who operate businesses online and how doing so can change the course of the company’s growth.Direct Access to a Confirmed PayPal Account Some of the s that make people engage in buying PayPal accounts include; First, PPDs end up getting acceses to instant account that has been verified. It can take sometimes to open a PayPal account, especially for those firms that require a verified account to process large amounts of money. Identification and linking accounts with the bank, as well as credit cards, takes several days or even weeks to complete to become activated for use in PayPal. Such PayPal accounts offer pre-verified accounts to avoid time wastage when trying to start using a PayPal account to receive payment.Boosting Business Credibility A PayPal account gives an added legitimacy to any online business. If customers come across PayPal logo, they are relieved knowing that they are dealing with one of the most secure payment systems globally. Whenever these new businesses are starting up, they can purchase already verified PayPal accounts since they have a professional outlook. First time customers enjoy the convenience of purchasing from businesses that use PayPal as a payment methodology since they trust it. In a way this can assist in developing trusted relationship and customers relationships in the long run.Access to Global Markets Perhaps the greatest benefit of acquiring PayPal accounts is probably the opening up of global markets. With the PayPal account, businesses in the global market can accept payments in more than two hundred countries. Ideally, for an e-commerce business, accept PayPal to open an account that helps you sell your products to clients all over the world. Acquiring a PayPal account offers an opportunity to locate a client base that is worldwide, thus infiltrating international markets which would be very effective in scaling the business.Seamless Integration with other Online Market Systems Since PayPal is recognized almost everywhere, it can be easily integrated with multiple online platforms, including mid and large online shops, selling platforms such as eBay, an internet shop platform like Shopify, and Amazon. This proves that purchasing a PayPal account guarantees your business the ability to include payment systems and keep your business ahead. It helps also in making the check out process easy for customers and therefore minimizing the number of abandoned carts. Furthermore, PayPal has buyer protection for clients this increases their confidence that their purchase is secure thus increasing the total sales for your business.Increased Transaction Limits PayPal has limitations when a user first opens a new PayPal account especially as regards sending or receiving money. Such limitations can be somewhat constraining, most especially for those organizations that require voluminous transactions daily. There are certain initial restrictions, as they, for example, limit the number of transactions a newly created PayPal account can perform per day, week or per month, but, in fact, by buying a verified PayPal account, businesses are already opened to higher transaction limits than how many they can execute per certain timeframe when they sign up for PayPal. This is especially so for the business organizations those deal with many sales since they can process the payments intact.Why Do You Buy PayPal Accounts? While accepting money online has become a part of people’s daily routine in the world of the Internet today. Depending on what type of services or products you are offering, reliable and safe payment methods do matter, no matter if you are a small business owner or an e-shopper or an employee who works in international freelance market. Starting with payment gateways, PayPal is probably one of the most famous and reliable company for making payments globally. However, the rising interest in purchasing PayPal accounts begs the question: What makes people purchase the PayPal accounts?

item.title: LangGraph + DeepSeek-R1 + Function Call + Agentic RAG (Insane Results)
item.author: Gao Dalie (高達烈)
item.content:encoded:
In this Story, I have a super quick tutorial showing you how to create a multi-agent chatbot using LangGraph, Deepseek-R1, function calling, and Agentic RAG to build a powerful agent chatbot for your business or personal use.In my last Story, I talked about LangChain and Deepseek-R1. One of the viewers asked if I could do it with LangGraph.I have fulfilled that request — but not only that, I have also enhanced the chatbot with function calling and Agentic RAG.“But Gao, Deepseek-R1 doesn’t support function calls!”Yes, you’re right — but let me tell you, I came up with a clever idea. If you stay until the end of the video, I’ll show you how to do it, too!I have mentioned the function call many times in my previous article, we already know that the function call is a technique that allows LLM to autonomously select and call predefined functions based on the conversation content. These functions can be used to perform various tasks.Agentic RAG is a type of RAG that improves the problems of general RAG by using agents. The problem with conventional RAG is that the AI ​​processes everything “in order”, so if an error occurs, such as not being able to retrieve data, all subsequent processing becomes meaningless.Agentic RAG addresses these issues by using an “agent.” The agent not only chooses the tool to go to but also allows for thought loops, so it can loop through multiple processes until it gets the information it needs and meets the user’s expectations.So, let me give you a quick demo of a live chatbot to show you what I mean.We have two different databases, research and development, where we can get our answers. I have created some example questions that you can test. Let me enter the question: ‘What is the status of Project A?’ If you look at how the chatbot generates the output,It uses RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter to split a text into smaller chunks and convert it into a document using splitter.create_documents(). These documents were stored as vector embeddings in ChromaDB for efficient similarity search.A retriever was created using create_retriever_tool in LangChain, and an AI agent was developed to classify user queries as research or development, connect to DeepSeek-R1 (temperature = 0.7) for responses, and retrieve relevant documents.One of the big problems I faced when I developed a chatbot was that DeepSeek-R1 does not support function calling like OpenAI, so I created a text-based command system instead of using function calls. Rather than forcing DeepSeek-R1 to use functions directly, I designed it to output specific text formats.Then, I built a wrapper to convert these text commands into tool actions. This way, we get the same functionality as function calling, but in a way, DeepSeek-R1 can handle it.A grading function checks if retrieved documents exist — if found, the process continues; if not, the query is rewritten for clarity. A generation function summarizes retrieved documents using DeepSeek-R1 and formats the response.A decision function determines whether tools are needed based on the message content, and a LangGraph workflow was created to manage the process, starting with the agent, routing queries to retrieval if needed, generating an answer when documents are found, or rewriting the question and retrying if necessary. Finally, it generates the final answer in a green box.By the end of this video, you will understand what is Agentic RAG, why we need Agentic RAG and how a function call + DeepSeek-R1 can be used to create a super AI Agent.If you like this topic and you want to support me:like my article; that will really help me out.👏Follow me on my YouTube channelSubscribe to me to get the latest articles.Agentic RAG is an RAG that integrates the capabilities of Agent, and the core capabilities of Agent are autonomous reasoning and action.Therefore, Agentic RAG brings the autonomous planning capabilities of AI agents (such as routing, action steps, reflection, etc.) into traditional RAG to adapt to more complex RAG query tasks.RAG = LLM + Knowledge Base + RetrieverAgentic RAG's “agent” feature is mainly reflected in the retrieval stage. Compared to the retrieval process in traditional RAG, Agentic RAG is more capable of:Deciding whether to search (autonomous decision-making)Choosing which search engine to use (autonomous planning)Evaluating the retrieved context and deciding whether to re-search (self-planning)Determining whether to use external toolsAgentic RAG VS Traditional RAGNow, let’s get on with the guide on how to build an AI chatbot using LangGraph + DeepSeek-R1 + Function Call + Agentic RAG. When it comes to creating this chatbot, we will create an ideal environment for the code to work. We need to install the necessary Python libraries. For this, we will do a pip install of the libraries below. pip install -r requirements.txt Once installed, we import the important dependencies like langchian, Langgraph and Streamlitfrom langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings from langchain_community.vectorstores import Chroma from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, AIMessage, ToolMessage from langchain.text_splitter import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter from langgraph.graph import END, StateGraph, START from langchain.tools.retriever import create_retriever_tool from langgraph.prebuilt import ToolNode from langgraph.graph.message import add_messages from typing_extensions import TypedDict, Annotated from typing import Sequence from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings import re import os import requests import streamlit as st I created two databases. The first set is called research_texts, and it includes some research-related statements, like reports and papers about AI and machine learning.The second set, development_texts, includes statements about ongoing projects, such as UI design, testing features, and optimization before a product release.But feel free to customize the data depending on your use case# Create Dummy Data research_texts = [ "Research Report: Results of a New AI Model Improving Image Recognition Accuracy to 98%", "Academic Paper Summary: Why Transformers Became the Mainstream Architecture in Natural Language Processing", "Latest Trends in Machine Learning Methods Using Quantum Computing" ] development_texts = [ "Project A: UI Design Completed, API Integration in Progress", "Project B: Testing New Feature X, Bug Fixes Needed", "Product Y: In the Performance Optimization Stage Before Release" ] Let’s process the data by splitting it into smaller parts, converting it into document objects, and then creating vector embeddings.I used the RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter to break long pieces of text into smaller chunks, ensuring that each chunk has a maximum of 100 characters.Then, I converted the split texts into document objects using splitter.create_documents(). These document objects will be useful later when working with Deepseek_R1 for searchingText splitting settings splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter(chunk_size=100, chunk_overlap=10) Generate Document objects from text research_docs = splitter.create_documents(research_texts) development_docs = splitter.create_documents(development_texts) Create vector stores embeddings = OpenAIEmbeddings( model="text-embedding-3-large", # With the `text-embedding-3` class # of models, you can specify the size # of the embeddings you want returned. # dimensions=1024 ) We store research and development documents as vector embeddings and make them searchable using retrievers.First, I use Chroma.from_documents() to create a vector store for research_docs, where the embedding=embeddings converts text into numerical vectors, and collection_name="research_collection" ensures the documents are stored in a dedicated collection for efficient retrieval.Once both research and development texts are stored as vectors we use retrieval to find and retrieve the most relevant document from a vector store.research_vectorstore = Chroma.from_documents( documents=research_docs, embedding=embeddings, collection_name="research_collection" ) development_vectorstore = Chroma.from_documents( documents=development_docs, embedding=embeddings, collection_name="development_collection" ) research_retriever = research_vectorstore.as_retriever() development_retriever = development_vectorstore.as_retriever() Then, we provide an AI agent with a tool, You first need to create a retriever and then import the tool.I use the create_retriever_tool function in LangChain, which creates a tool for retrieving documents. The documents retrieved by this tool can be extracted from the return value of the function that this tool wraps,research_tool = create_retriever_tool( research_retriever, # Retriever object "research_db_tool", # Name of the tool to create "Search information from the research database." # Description of the tool ) development_tool = create_retriever_tool( development_retriever, "development_db_tool", "Search information from the development database." ) # Combine the created research and development tools into a list tools = [research_tool, development_tool] I developed an agent function as a smart router for user questions. I extract the user’s message and use a prompt to categorize it as either research or development-related.I connect to DeepSeek AI with a temperature of 0.7 for balanced responses. When the API responds, I check if it’s a research or development query, then use the appropriate retriever to find relevant documents. I format everything into a standard message format, including both the query and results.If it doesn’t fit either category, I return a direct answer. Think of it as a traffic controller — directing questions to the right database for the best answers. def agent(state: AgentState): print("---CALL AGENT---") messages = state["messages"] if isinstance(messages[0], tuple): user_message = messages[0][1] else: user_message = messages[0].content # Structure prompt for consistent text output prompt = f"""Given this user question: "{user_message}" If it's about research or academic topics, respond EXACTLY in this format: SEARCH_RESEARCH: If it's about development status, respond EXACTLY in this format: SEARCH_DEV: Otherwise, just answer directly. """ headers = { "Accept": "application/json", "Authorization": f"Bearer sk-1cddf19f9dc4466fa3ecea6fe10abec0", "Content-Type": "application/json" } data = { "model": "deepseek-chat", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}], "temperature": 0.7, "max_tokens": 1024 } response = requests.post( "https://api.deepseek.com/v1/chat/completions", headers=headers, json=data, verify=False ) if response.status_code == 200: response_text = response.json()['choices'][0]['message']['content'] print("Raw response:", response_text) # Format the response into expected tool format if "SEARCH_RESEARCH:" in response_text: query = response_text.split("SEARCH_RESEARCH:")[1].strip() # Use direct call to research retriever results = research_retriever.invoke(query) return {"messages": [AIMessage(content=f'Action: research_db_tool\n{{"query": "{query}"}}\n\nResults: {str(results)}')]} elif "SEARCH_DEV:" in response_text: query = response_text.split("SEARCH_DEV:")[1].strip() # Use direct call to development retriever results = development_retriever.invoke(query) return {"messages": [AIMessage(content=f'Action: development_db_tool\n{{"query": "{query}"}}\n\nResults: {str(results)}')]} else: return {"messages": [AIMessage(content=response_text)]} else: raise Exception(f"API call failed: {response.text}") Then, we designed a grading function that checks if our search was successful. I look at the last message received and check if it contains any retrieved documents.If I find documents (marked by “Results: [Document”), I signal to move forward with generating an answer.If I don’t find any documents, I suggest rewriting the question for better results. It’s like a quality check — making sure we have the right materials before proceeding.def simple_grade_documents(state: AgentState): messages = state["messages"] last_message = messages[-1] print("Evaluating message:", last_message.content) # Check if the content contains retrieved documents if "Results: [Document" in last_message.content: print("---DOCS FOUND, GO TO GENERATE---") return "generate" else: print("---NO DOCS FOUND, TRY REWRITE---") return "rewrite" I developed a generation function that creates the final answer from our found documents. I start by getting the original question and any documents we found in our search.I extract the document content and create a prompt that asks DeepSeek-R1 to summarize the findings, focusing on key research advancements. I set up the API call with specific headers and a temperature of 0.7 for balanced creativity.Once I get a response, I format it into a user-friendly message. If something goes wrong with the API call, It let you know about the error.def generate(state: AgentState): print("---GENERATE FINAL ANSWER---") messages = state["messages"] question = messages[0].content if isinstance(messages[0], tuple) else messages[0].content last_message = messages[-1] # Extract the document content from the results docs = "" if "Results: [" in last_message.content: results_start = last_message.content.find("Results: [") docs = last_message.content[results_start:] print("Documents found:", docs) headers = { "Accept": "application/json", "Authorization": f"Bearer sk-1cddf19f9dc4466fa3ecea6fe10abec0", "Content-Type": "application/json" } prompt = f"""Based on these research documents, summarize the latest advancements in AI: Question: {question} Documents: {docs} Focus on extracting and synthesizing the key findings from the research papers. """ data = { "model": "deepseek-chat", "messages": [{ "role": "user", "content": prompt }], "temperature": 0.7, "max_tokens": 1024 } print("Sending generate request to API...") response = requests.post( "https://api.deepseek.com/v1/chat/completions", headers=headers, json=data, verify=False ) if response.status_code == 200: response_text = response.json()['choices'][0]['message']['content'] print("Final Answer:", response_text) return {"messages": [AIMessage(content=response_text)]} else: raise Exception(f"API call failed: {response.text}") I designed a rewrite function that helps improve unclear questions. I take the original question and ask DeepSeek to make it more specific and clearer. I set up the API call with custom headers and a balanced temperature of 0.7, then asked the model to rewrite the question. When I get a response, I check if it worked properly — if it did, I return the improved question; if not, I will print the errordef rewrite(state: AgentState): print("---REWRITE QUESTION---") messages = state["messages"] original_question = messages[0].content if len(messages)>0 else "N/A" headers = { "Accept": "application/json", "Authorization": f"Bearer sk-1cddf19f9dc4466fa3ecea6fe10abec0", "Content-Type": "application/json" } data = { "model": "deepseek-chat", "messages": [{ "role": "user", "content": f"Rewrite this question to be more specific and clearer: {original_question}" }], "temperature": 0.7, "max_tokens": 1024 } print("Sending rewrite request...") response = requests.post( "https://api.deepseek.com/v1/chat/completions", headers=headers, json=data, verify=False ) print("Status Code:", response.status_code) print("Response:", response.text) if response.status_code == 200: response_text = response.json()['choices'][0]['message']['content'] print("Rewritten question:", response_text) return {"messages": [AIMessage(content=response_text)]} else: raise Exception(f"API call failed: {response.text}") I created a decision-making function that checks if we need to use any tools based on the message content. I look at the last message and check if it matches our tool pattern (which looks for “Action:” at the start).If it matches, I signal that we should retrieve information using our tools. If it doesn’t match, I signal that we should end the process.def custom_tools_condition(state: AgentState): messages = state["messages"] last_message = messages[-1] content = last_message.content print("Checking tools condition:", content) if tools_pattern.match(content): print("Moving to retrieve...") return "tools" print("Moving to END...") return END Let’s create a workflow system that connects all our functions in a logical sequence. I used a LangGraph where each function is a node, starting with the agent that first receives the question. I set up special paths using edges — when the agent needs tools, it goes to retrieve information; if not, it ends. After retrieving, I check the documents: good results go to generate an answer, while poor results go back to rewrite the questionworkflow = StateGraph(AgentState) # Define the workflow using StateGraph workflow.add_node("agent", agent) retrieve_node = ToolNode(tools) workflow.add_node("retrieve", retrieve_node) workflow.add_node("rewrite", rewrite) workflow.add_node("generate", generate) # Define nodes workflow.add_edge(START, "agent") # If the agent calls a tool, proceed to retrieve; otherwise, go to END workflow.add_conditional_edges( "agent", custom_tools_condition, { "tools": "retrieve", END: END } ) # After retrieval, determine whether to generate or rewrite workflow.add_conditional_edges("retrieve", simple_grade_documents) workflow.add_edge("generate", END) workflow.add_edge("rewrite", "agent") # Compile the workflow to make it executable app = workflow.compile() Let’s make a function that manages how questions flow through our system. I take a user’s question and process it through the workflow app, collecting every event (like searching, finding documents, or generating answers) into a list.def process_question(user_question, app, config): """Process user question through the workflow""" events = [] for event in app.stream({"messages":[("user", user_question)]}, config): events.append(event) return events Then, we use Streamlit to create a clean layout and put database content in a sidebar for easy reference. In the main area, I created a text input box for questions and split the screen into two columns. In the first column, I handle the question processing: when you click the “Get Answer” button, I show a loading spinner and process your question through the workflow.I display any found documents in expandable sections and show the final answer with a green success box. In the second column, I added helpful instructions and example questions.def main(): st.set_page_config( page_title="AI Research & Development Assistant", layout="wide", initial_sidebar_state="expanded" ) # Custom CSS st.markdown(""" """, unsafe_allow_html=True) # Sidebar with Data Display with st.sidebar: st.header("📚 Available Data") st.subheader("Research Database") for text in research_texts: st.markdown(f'
{text}
', unsafe_allow_html=True) st.subheader("Development Database") for text in development_texts: st.markdown(f'
{text}
', unsafe_allow_html=True) # Main Content st.title("🤖 AI Research & Development Assistant") st.markdown("---") # Query Input query = st.text_area("Enter your question:", height=100, placeholder="e.g., What is the latest advancement in AI research?") col1, col2 = st.columns([1,2]) with col1: if st.button("🔍 Get Answer", use_container_width=True): if query: with st.spinner('Processing your question...'): # Process query through workflow events = process_question(query, app, {"configurable":{"thread_id":"1"}}) # Display results for event in events: if 'agent' in event: with st.expander("🔄 Processing Step", expanded=True): content = event['agent']['messages'][0].content if "Results:" in content: # Display retrieved documents st.markdown("### 📑 Retrieved Documents:") docs_start = content.find("Results:") docs = content[docs_start:] st.info(docs) elif 'generate' in event: st.markdown("### ✨ Final Answer:") st.success(event['generate']['messages'][0].content) else: st.warning("⚠️ Please enter a question first!") with col2: st.markdown(""" ### 🎯 How to Use 1. Type your question in the text box 2. Click "Get Answer" to process 3. View retrieved documents and final answer ### 💡 Example Questions - What are the latest advancements in AI research? - What is the status of Project A? - What are the current trends in machine learning? """) if __name__ == "__main__": main() Agentic RAG and DeepSeek-R1 Function Call are innovative information retrieval and generation technologies that can take action autonomously. They can even complete complex tasks by breaking them down into smaller steps and calling the appropriate tools when needed, pushing the boundaries of AI in real-time decision-making and dynamic content generation. As these technologies evolve, their applications will become more diverse, driving innovation across numerous industries.If this article might be helpful to your friends, please forward it to them.🧙‍♂️ I am an !
